ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for Sambar Vadai exists. Mapped to 'fritter (food product)' as the closest defensible parent category for a deep-fried lentil cake served in lentil soup.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Sambar Vadai. A0CKP (legume-based snack, deep-fried) is the closest defensible parent anchor. Exact match unavailable.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Sambar Vadai or a sufficiently close analogue in the clinical terminology system.

allergens: none identified (naturally gluten-free if no wheat added)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Sambar Vadai in Singapore consists of 2 vadai pieces soaked in sambar, weighing approximately 200 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Sambar Vadai — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Sambar Vadai (cr_id G00011050). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical entry with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Lentil-based Foods — Analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database: Lentils (boiled), GI ~29–41; Medu Vada / fried lentil cake analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry for Sambar Vadai exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 41 based on the closest analogous approved-source entries: cooked urad dal / lentils (GI 29–41, University of Sydney GI Database) and the moderating effect of high dietary fibre (4.0 g/100g) and protein (4.33 g/100g). Soaking in sambar further moderates glycaemic response. Glycemic load calculated as: GI (41) × carbohydrate per serving (23.54 g for 200 g serving) / 100 = 9.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium (207 mg/100g) offset by high potassium (237 mg/100g) and dietary fibre (4.0 g/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Sambar Vadai has a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio and a high-fibre lentil base consistent with DASH dietary principles; individuals with hypertension should limit portion to 1–2 vadai pieces and request reduced-salt sambar preparation to keep total sodium intake controlled.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low estimated GI (~41), high dietary fibre (4.0 g/100g), and moderate protein (4.33 g/100g) supporting glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: The high dietary fibre from lentils and vegetables slows glucose absorption, and the low estimated GI of approximately 41 makes Sambar Vadai a suitable occasional choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when portion is limited to 1–2 pieces (approximately 100–150 g) to manage total carbohydrate load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation adds fat; however, low saturated fat (~0.5 g/100g), zero cholesterol, and high dietary fibre (4.0 g/100g) are cardioprotective
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The lentil base provides soluble fibre and plant protein beneficial for cardiovascular health, but the deep-fried vadai component contributes total fat (2.71 g/100g); individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit to 1 piece per serving and prefer sambar-soaked vadai over freshly fried to reduce fat density.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that may worsen joint inflammation; anti-inflammatory spices (turmeric, cumin) in sambar partially offset this
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should be aware that deep-fried vadai generates AGEs that may exacerbate joint inflammation; soaking in sambar partially mitigates this, and those with nightshade sensitivity should request tomato-free sambar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sambar Vadai is a popular South Indian snack widely consumed in Singapore, consisting of deep-fried medu vadai (savoury lentil doughnuts made primarily from urad dal / black gram lentils) soaked in sambar, a spiced lentil and vegetable soup made with toor dal, tomatoes, tamarind, onions, and a blend of spices including turmeric, cumin, coriander, and sambar powder. The vadai is ground, shaped into a ring, and deep-fried until golden and crisp, then immersed in hot sambar, which softens the exterior while the interior remains dense and flavourful. The dish has a savoury, tangy, mildly spiced taste profile with a soft, spongy texture after soaking. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 86 kcal, 4.33 g protein, 2.71 g fat, 11.77 g carbohydrate, 4.0 g dietary fibre, and 207 mg sodium. The high fibre content from lentils and vegetables supports gut health and glycaemic regulation. The estimated glycemic index is approximately 41 (low), making it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose. It is naturally gluten-free, vegetarian, vegan, and halal. Clinically, it is considered low concern for hypertension and Type 2 Diabetes when portion-controlled, and moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to deep-frying. It is a common breakfast, lunch, or dinner item in Indian hawker stalls and restaurants across Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in sambar vadai?
Is sambar vadai good for diabetics?
What is sambar vadai made of?
Is sambar vadai suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00011050) with confidence 0.95. This is an exact match from the primary approved regional source for Singapore dishes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using lentil-based food entries, clearly documented.
notes: 1. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 by HPB (confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database for this dish — the vadai is made from lentils with no animal-derived ingredients). 2. saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.5 g/100g based on typical deep-frying oil contribution for lentil-based fritters; HPB source does not separately report saturated fat for this entry — this is an adapted estimate. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for Sambar Vadai are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this dish as of 2026-04-16. This information is not available from the primary source.
